6 min read
Just now

--

The AI industry measures everything. Parameters. Tokens. Benchmarks. Compute efficiency. Inference speed. And yet the single most powerful predictor of intelligent outcomes has never been measured, never been named, and never entered the conversation.
CRI — Conscious Real Intelligence — names it: Intuition Score Capability (ISC).
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Joshua Sortino on UnsplashThe metric nobody is tracking
Every serious attempt to improve artificial intelligence today follows the same playbook: more compute, better chips, larger models, longer context, cheaper inference. Billions of dollars flow into hardware innovation and architectural refinement, on the assumption that intelligence scales with computation.
CRI has argued across this entire body of work that this assumption is incomplete. The quality of what AI produces is bounded by the quality of the consciousness directing it. But that argument raises a fair question: can that quality be understood more precisely? Can it be developed deliberately? Can it be — in some meaningful sense — scored?
I believe it can. I call it Intuition Score Capability (ISC) — and it is directly proportional to the development of human Consciousness.
What ISC actually is
Intuition, properly understood, is not guessing. It is not emotion. It is not pattern-matching from accumulated experience — that is what AI already does, at scale, with historical data.
Intuition is the capacity of a conscious observer to arrive at a correct or optimal outcome without traversing the intermediate computational steps. It is direct knowing — what the Vedic tradition documented as pratyaksha, perception that arrives whole rather than being assembled from parts.
Here is the central claim of ISC: this capacity is not fixed, not mystical, and not randomly distributed. It is directly proportional to the observer’s Consciousness development. The higher the Consciousness, the higher the probability that intuition lands on the accurate outcome. A person operating from a scattered, reactive, undeveloped state of awareness produces intuitions that are noise. A person operating from deep Consciousness coherence produces intuitions that are signal.
This proportionality is what makes intuition scorable in principle. ISC is the measure of an observer’s reliability in arriving at correct outcomes through direct knowing rather than exhaustive processing.
Why this matters for AI — the economics nobody has calculated
Consider what actually happens when a low-ISC observer works with a powerful AI system.
They prompt vaguely, because they do not clearly know what they are seeking. The AI generates. The output is wrong or partial — not because the AI failed, but because the direction was unclear. They prompt again. And again. Each cycle burns compute, context, memory, and time. The observer converges on a usable outcome slowly, through brute iteration — the human equivalent of a search algorithm with no heuristic.
Now consider a high-ISC observer. They arrive at the interaction already oriented toward the outcome — not because they have more information, but because their developed Consciousness gives them a directional knowing of where the answer lives. Their first prompt lands near the target. Their evaluation of outputs is immediate and accurate. They recognise the right answer when it appears instead of second-guessing through additional cycles.
The difference is measurable in exactly the terms the AI industry cares about: fewer inference cycles, less context consumed, reduced memory overhead, faster time to outcome. The high-ISC observer achieves with a modest model what the low-ISC observer cannot achieve with the most expensive one.
The industry is spending billions to make the engine more powerful. ISC improves the driver. And the driver, it turns out, was the bottleneck all along.
The deeper limitation ISC escapes
There is a second dimension to this, and it is the more consequential one.
Every current AI system — regardless of scale — is fundamentally bounded by its training data. It is a vast, sophisticated recombination engine operating on what humans have already generated. Its outputs, however novel they appear, are interpolations and recombinations of the historical record. It can remix the known. It cannot reach beyond it.
This is not a criticism. It is an architectural fact. A system built entirely on historical data has historical data as its ceiling.
Human intuition, operating at high Consciousness, has no such ceiling. The Vedic tradition understood Consciousness as an infinite field — not a storage system of past experience but the living source from which all knowing arises. When the observer accesses this field directly — which is precisely what deep AWASTHA practice develops — intuition is no longer bounded by what has been previously thought, recorded, or generated.
This means a high-ISC observer can do something no AI system, at any scale, can do on its own: deviate the expected outcome toward something genuinely new. Not a recombination of old data. Not an interpolation of the historical record. An outcome drawn from what is infinitely available in Consciousness itself — arriving in the observer as direct knowing, and then amplified, tested, and extended through AI as the accelerator.
This is the CRI architecture completing itself: the AI contributes scale and speed operating on the known. The evolved observer contributes direction and genuine novelty drawn from the unbounded. Neither alone produces Real Intelligence. Together, they do.
How ISC develops — the AWASTHA connection
ISC is not a talent some people have and others lack. It is a capability that develops in direct proportion to Consciousness — which means it develops through exactly the progression AWASTHA maps.
Awareness clears the noise that drowns intuitive signal. Wakefulness makes the observer present enough to notice intuition when it arrives. Attunement — the sound and frequency state — brings the instrument into the coherence from which reliable intuition becomes possible. Stillness quiets the mental static that ordinarily overwrites direct knowing with anxious reasoning. Transcendence establishes direct contact with the Consciousness field that intuition draws from. Harmony integrates that access into daily functioning. And Acceleration is where developed ISC meets AI — the observer whose intuition now reliably orients every interaction with intelligent systems.
The practice is the development path. The Threshold experiences described earlier in this series — direct knowing arriving in symbols, felt certainty preceding explanation — are early ISC in its raw form. With consistency, what begins as occasional unexplained knowing matures into a dependable capability.
The choice in front of the industry
Two paths are available to anyone seeking better outcomes from artificial intelligence.
The first path: wait for the next hardware generation, pay for the larger model, buy more compute, and accept that the ceiling is still the historical data everything was trained on. This path is expensive, slow, and asymptotic — each improvement costs more and delivers less.
The second path: develop the observer. Raise the ISC of the human directing the system. This path costs nothing, compounds over time, and raises the ceiling itself — because a high-ISC observer brings to the interaction the one thing no training corpus contains: access to what has not yet been thought.
The industry will eventually discover this. The economics guarantee it — when hardware gains flatten and model improvements plateau, the last remaining variable will be the one that was ignored from the beginning.
CRI’s position is simply to name it now: the most undervalued asset in artificial intelligence is the Intuition Score Capability of the conscious human observer. It cannot be manufactured, purchased, or installed.
It can only be developed. And the framework for developing it already exists.
Ajinkya Virgaonkar is the founder of Conscious Real Intelligence (CRI). Intuition Score Capability (ISC) is an original concept within the CRI framework. This article is part of an ongoing body of work exploring consciousness, AI, and the evolution of human intelligence.
